Philosophical schools of thought such as deontology (which emphasizes duty and universal moral laws), consequentialism (focusing on the outcomes of actions), and virtue ethics (centered on character traits) offer frameworks for ethical decision-making without relying on divine authority.

In modern times, people from diverse religious backgrounds or those who do not affiliate with any religion can still adhere to moral principles, engage in ethical behavior, and form moral communities based on shared values and rational discussions.
